title: Allergy & Immunology
subtitle: From Hypersensitivity Foundations to Advanced Immunologic Reasoning
author: Lauren Fine, MD, FAAAAI
description: A comprehensive clinical reasoning pathway spanning foundational immunology through advanced allergy/immunology practice. Designed for medical students through A/I fellows with tiered difficulty levels.

modules.title: Hypersensitivity & Immune Foundations
modules.subtitle: The immunologic framework every clinician needs
modules.description: Master the Gell-Coombs classification, understand IgE-mediated vs non-IgE pathways, and build the mechanistic foundation for clinical allergy reasoning.

activities.title: Gell-Coombs Classification & Immune Mechanisms
activities.description: Type I–IV hypersensitivity reactions, key mediators, and clinical correlates.
objectives: Classify hypersensitivity reactions by Gell-Coombs type
Identify key mediators in each reaction type
Predict clinical presentation from immune mechanism

activities.title: Allergy Testing: Principles & Interpretation
activities.description: Skin prick, intradermal, specific IgE, component testing, and when to use each.
objectives: Select appropriate allergy test for the clinical scenario
Interpret skin prick and serum IgE results
Recognize false positives and false negatives

activities.title: CRT: Allergy Testing Decision-Making
activities.description: Step-by-step reasoning through test selection, interpretation, and clinical correlation.
objectives: Apply Bayesian reasoning to allergy test interpretation
Navigate the skin test → serum IgE → challenge testing algorithm

activities.title: Interactive: Allergy Testing Module
activities.description: Hands-on allergy testing interpretation with case-based questions.
objectives: Practice interpreting skin prick results
Correlate test results with clinical history

modules.title: Anaphylaxis: Recognition, Management & Prevention
modules.subtitle: The most time-critical diagnosis in allergy
modules.description: From recognizing biphasic reactions to managing refractory anaphylaxis and understanding food-dependent exercise-induced presentations. Covers the full spectrum from ED recognition to outpatient prevention planning.

activities.title: Anaphylaxis Fundamentals
activities.description: NIAID/FAAN criteria, epinephrine dosing, biphasic reactions, and observation periods.
objectives: Apply NIAID diagnostic criteria for anaphylaxis
Dose and administer epinephrine correctly
Identify risk factors for biphasic anaphylaxis

activities.title: Case: Anaphylaxis — Priya Sharma
activities.description: 27-year-old female with acute allergic reaction and airway compromise. Full EMR encounter.
objectives: Recognize anaphylaxis in the ED
Manage acute anaphylaxis with appropriate orders
Document and disposition appropriately

activities.title: AI Trainer: Anaphylaxis & Food Allergy
activities.description: AI-guided clinical reasoning through anaphylaxis scenarios with real-time feedback.
objectives: Practice anaphylaxis management with AI coaching
Receive feedback on clinical reasoning patterns

activities.title: CRT: Anaphylaxis — Expanded Reasoning
activities.description: Complex anaphylaxis scenarios: refractory anaphylaxis, beta-blocker use, mast cell disorders.
objectives: Manage refractory anaphylaxis
Navigate anaphylaxis in patients on beta-blockers
Recognize mast cell activation as a mimic/comorbidity

activities.title: CRT: Anaphylaxis — Dual Challenge Adventure
activities.description: Advanced: food-dependent exercise-induced anaphylaxis, cofactor-augmented reactions, idiopathic anaphylaxis workup.
objectives: Diagnose food-dependent exercise-induced anaphylaxis
Develop a systematic workup for idiopathic anaphylaxis
Apply mastocytosis screening criteria

activities.title: Pediatric Anaphylaxis
activities.description: Age-specific recognition challenges, weight-based dosing, school action plans.
objectives: Recognize atypical anaphylaxis presentations in children
Calculate pediatric epinephrine dosing
Write an anaphylaxis emergency action plan

activities.title: Case: Severe Allergic Reaction — Thomas Rivera
activities.description: 48-year-old male with anaphylaxis versus severe allergic reaction. Diagnostic distinction with management implications.

modules.title: Angioedema: Beyond Simple Allergy
modules.subtitle: ACE inhibitor, hereditary, and idiopathic angioedema
modules.description: The angioedema differential is broad and the management varies dramatically by etiology. This module builds the reasoning to distinguish mast-cell-mediated from bradykinin-mediated angioedema — a distinction that saves lives.

activities.title: Urticaria & Angioedema Overview
activities.description: Acute vs chronic urticaria, angioedema classification, when to worry about underlying cause.
objectives: Classify urticaria as acute vs chronic
Distinguish histaminergic from bradykinin-mediated angioedema
Identify red flags requiring further workup

activities.title: Chronic Urticaria & Angioedema
activities.description: Chronic spontaneous urticaria, omalizumab, urticaria activity scores, step-up therapy.
objectives: Apply the CSU treatment algorithm
Counsel patients on omalizumab
Use UAS7 to monitor disease activity

activities.title: Case: ACE Inhibitor Angioedema — Dorothy Washington
activities.description: 67-year-old female with lip and tongue swelling on lisinopril. Bradykinin pathway, icatibant, airway management.

activities.title: Case: Hereditary Angioedema — Nicole Dubois
activities.description: 31-year-old female with recurrent angioedema episodes without urticaria. C1-INH deficiency workup and on-demand therapy.

activities.title: Case: Acquired C1-Esterase Inhibitor Deficiency — Harold Jefferson
activities.description: 69-year-old male with late-onset angioedema. Lymphoproliferative disease workup, C1-INH vs C4 interpretation.

activities.title: Case: Allergic Angioedema — Priya Nair
activities.description: 35-year-old female with allergic angioedema — contrast with non-allergic presentations.

activities.title: Case: Idiopathic Angioedema — Diana Castillo-Perez
activities.description: 43-year-old female with recurrent angioedema, negative workup. Diagnosis of exclusion and management strategy.

modules.title: Drug Allergy, Desensitization & Drug Reactions
modules.subtitle: Penicillin myths, DRESS, SJS/TEN, and safe challenge protocols
modules.description: Drug allergy is the most commonly over-diagnosed condition in medicine. This module teaches the systematic approach from history-taking to challenge protocols, and covers the severe cutaneous adverse reactions that demand urgent recognition.

activities.title: Drug Allergy Fundamentals
activities.description: True drug allergy vs adverse effect, penicillin cross-reactivity, the de-labeling revolution.
objectives: Distinguish IgE-mediated drug allergy from adverse drug reaction
Apply current penicillin cross-reactivity data
Identify candidates for penicillin skin testing and direct challenge

activities.title: Case: DRESS Syndrome — Marcus Jefferson
activities.description: 37-year-old male on allopurinol with fever, rash, and eosinophilia. RegiSCAR scoring, HLA associations.

activities.title: Case: SJS/TEN — Keisha Brown
activities.description: 40-year-old female with mucosal erosions and skin detachment. SCORTEN prognosis, culprit identification, burn unit criteria.

activities.title: Case: SJS/TEN Overlap — Ananya Patel
activities.description: 33-year-old female with overlap syndrome. Body surface area assessment, IVIG controversy, long-term follow-up.

activities.title: Case: Erythema Multiforme Major — Tyler Brennan
activities.description: 24-year-old male with target lesions and mucosal involvement. Distinguishing EM from SJS.

activities.title: Case: Neutropenic Fever with Penicillin Allergy
activities.description: Navigating anti-pseudomonal coverage in a patient labeled 'penicillin anaphylaxis.' Carbapenem safety data, cross-reactivity, and when to challenge.

modules.title: Food Allergy & Oral Immunotherapy
modules.subtitle: IgE-mediated food allergy, FPIES, and emerging therapies
modules.description: Food allergy affects 8% of children and is increasing in prevalence. This module covers the clinical spectrum from diagnosis through oral food challenge and into the emerging landscape of oral immunotherapy and biologics.

activities.title: Food Allergy Overview
activities.description: IgE-mediated food allergy, FPIES, OAS/PFS, EoE, component resolved diagnostics.
objectives: Classify food allergy phenotypes
Interpret component testing for peanut and tree nuts
Counsel families on allergen avoidance and emergency action plans

activities.title: AI Trainer: Food Allergy Reasoning
activities.description: AI-guided reasoning through food allergy diagnosis and anaphylaxis management.
objectives: Practice food allergy clinical reasoning with AI coaching

activities.title: CRT: Allergy Testing Adventure — Food Focus
activities.description: Advanced test interpretation for food allergy with probability curves and 95% PPV cutoffs.
objectives: Apply component resolved diagnostics to food allergy
Use probability curves for clinical decision-making

modules.title: Asthma: Phenotyping to Biologics
modules.subtitle: From acute exacerbation to precision medicine
modules.description: Asthma management has been transformed by biologic therapies. This module covers the full spectrum: acute exacerbation management in the ED, step therapy, phenotyping/endotyping, and biologic selection.

activities.title: Asthma Fundamentals
activities.description: Pathophysiology, classification, step therapy, inhaler technique, action plans.
objectives: Classify asthma severity and control
Apply GINA/NAEPP step therapy
Write an asthma action plan

activities.title: Case: Asthma Exacerbation — Deshawn Carter
activities.description: 24-year-old male with acute severe asthma. Continuous nebs, IV magnesium, intubation criteria.

activities.title: Asthma Phenotyping & Biologics
activities.description: T2-high vs T2-low, biomarker panels (FeNO, eosinophils, IgE), biologic selection algorithm.
objectives: Phenotype asthma using biomarkers
Select the appropriate biologic for each phenotype
Monitor biologic response

activities.title: Asthma Biologics Deep Dive
activities.description: Omalizumab, mepolizumab, benralizumab, dupilumab, tezepelumab — mechanism, data, selection, and switching strategies.
objectives: Compare biologic mechanisms and trial data
Develop a biologic switching strategy
Navigate biologic overlap indications (asthma + CRSwNP + AD)

activities.title: Case: Vocal Cord Dysfunction — Alyssa Chen
activities.description: 22-year-old female with 'refractory asthma' that's actually VCD. The great mimicker.

modules.title: Immunodeficiency: When to Suspect & Workup
modules.subtitle: Primary immunodeficiency and the warning signs
modules.description: Immunodeficiency is underdiagnosed and often presents to multiple specialties before the correct diagnosis is made. This module teaches the systematic approach from clinical suspicion through advanced immunologic workup.

activities.title: Immunodeficiency Overview
activities.description: 10 warning signs, B-cell vs T-cell vs complement vs phagocyte defects, screening tests.
objectives: Recognize the 10 warning signs of immunodeficiency
Categorize immunodeficiency by affected arm of immunity
Order appropriate initial workup

activities.title: Primary Immunodeficiency Deep Dive
activities.description: CVID, XLA, SCID, CGD, complement deficiencies — diagnosis, management, IVIG/SCIG, and monitoring.
objectives: Diagnose common PIDs by clinical pattern
Initiate and monitor immunoglobulin replacement
Recognize when to refer for transplant evaluation

modules.title: Venom Allergy & Environmental/Occupational
modules.subtitle: Stinging insects, venom immunotherapy, and the workplace
modules.description: Venom allergy is one of the most preventable causes of anaphylaxis through venom immunotherapy. This module also covers occupational allergy and environmental exposures relevant to A/I practice.

activities.title: Venom Allergy & Immunotherapy
activities.description: Hymenoptera identification, venom testing, VIT protocols, duration, and special populations.
objectives: Identify candidates for venom immunotherapy
Select appropriate venom testing strategy
Manage VIT in patients on ACE inhibitors or beta-blockers

activities.title: Case: Coral Snake Envenomation — Derek Sullivan
activities.description: 39-year-old male with coral snake bite — neurotoxic envenomation, delayed symptom onset, antivenom timing.

modules.title: Integration: Cross-Cutting A/I Reasoning
modules.subtitle: Complex cases that span multiple A/I domains
modules.description: Real A/I practice requires integrating across domains. These cases require synthesizing knowledge from multiple modules — the way actual consultations work.

activities.title: Case: SLE Flare with Cytopenias — Aaliyah Jackson
activities.description: Immune-mediated disease flare requiring integration of immunology, hematology, and rheumatology reasoning.

activities.title: Case: Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia — Nadine Toussaint
activities.description: Warm AIHA — immune-mediated destruction, DAT interpretation, steroid management vs rituximab.

activities.title: Consult Callback Practice
activities.description: Practice calling an A/I consult — present a case, defend your reasoning, and get structured feedback.
objectives: Structure an A/I consult call
Present a focused allergy/immunology history

activities.title: CRT: A/I Comprehensive Reasoning
activities.description: The capstone clinical reasoning trainer covering the full A/I decision tree.
objectives: Navigate the complete A/I clinical reasoning framework
Integrate across hypersensitivity, immunodeficiency, and drug allergy domains
